like, you know, don't have unlimited, like, vacation time.

she still might be connecting.

it's crazy.

get all, they get all the tourists, they know when the demands up.

us.

yeah.

there.

exactly.

but yeah, I appreciate you both jumping on, um, Allison, I think you were talking with Ali.

What you know about us.

So we're always looking for market data and it just looked really comprehensive and good information.

Let me ask you this.

Do you guys?

So it's mostly from that standpoint
and you're using Kar right now.
That's correct.
Ok.
So I'll let you know we have a uh a brand new tool coming up that's actually better for, for what, for your use case.

But one of the reports that you got are probably from like more of the operational side where where basically if you have a specific property in mind, we generate like a market survey for it and give you a market rate data for all the competitors.
Um the new report when it's actually it's built, it's live our, our enterprise customers use it.
It's gonna be available for pretty much everybody within a couple of weeks here.
Um It's gonna, it's, it's, we call it like an investor tool.
It's like a proforma tool, it'll work bilaterally.
So if you want if you have a specific subject property in mind that you're looking to actually invest in, you can start there and we can give you market data on all of its competitors.
Or if you just say, hey, you know what we know, we wanna, we, we know we wanna make an investment in, in Phoenix.
You, we can get you market data for all of Phoenix.
Like let's say, you know, we're interested in class a properties um that are garden style communities that were built uh for 2015 and newer, we can give you all that market data.
And then from there, you can, you know, you know, you can, you can kind of zoom in or you get a much better scope.

So, you know, if you want to start at Phoenix, then you want to narrow it down to Scottsdale and you say, hey, you know, it's these three zip codes, you know, we can do all that so completely self serve from that perspective.
Um
That's probably what you're looking for more so than, than uh
kind of what I, what I can show you, I guess.
Yeah, I agree.
Yeah, that would be uh that would be, this is a separate demo with our product team.
I, I kind of catch hell all the time from our product team because they don't like me to talk about it before they have specific release dates, but they, they have the ability to show it to you.
So I, I, if, if we could set up some time early next week, I'd love for them to walk you through it.
I, I do just wanna show you a couple of things here really quick, which I think will be valuable and, and just so you get the idea of apartment IQ um everything we do is fully automated.
So all of our data collection is fully automated, which is great.
Um We update our data every single day and we can track any property out there as long as they have a website and use a property management software.
They do not have to be our customers whatsoever.
So long story short there, the way we get our data is we go out to the property websites every night and we get the data directly from the online leasing section of that property website.
And with that being said, everything that we present inside of Apartment IQ is 100% publicly available data.
Um I do like to mention that just in regards to the to the real page and now you already lawsuit centered around the sharing of, you know, private, semi private data.
So we, we do have nothing to do with that.
It's all 100% publicly available information.

Do you like Excel?
Do you have data visualization tools like Power B I tableau things like that or, or how do you prefer to kind of ingest the data
right now?

So that's what we know.
Cool.
Yeah.
So let me, so we can, if, if you prefer to continue working out of Excel, we can definitely, I mean, everything in here is downloadable to CS P or Excel.

The way this is set up right now is is it like I said, this is more from the operation side but each one, each property would actually get its own individual property card and then we'd run the database off of that property specific comp set and I'll let you know too.

What's nice is, is whenever we set these up, you know, you can give us a list of comps that you, you feel like you, you like to track or we also have a comp matching algorithm where we, where we can make suggestions on who we think are, are comparable properties um just based off of a of a certain algorithm.
So a couple of different ways to set those up.
But I mean, as far as a market survey goes, you know, this is pretty representative of a standard market survey.
So, so Dennis, you have your template basically and you, you're making phone calls right now and just filling out this information manually.

Yes, I try to pull everything I can from either Costar or the website and if there's anything missing, I call the property, usually I call them for occupancy because Costar is a little shaky with that.
Um But yeah,
OK.
Makes sense.
Yeah.
So, I mean, I was just automating that process.
And what's nice too is um from, from the uh the other, the uh the proforma tool that I was talking about, you can actually click and generate these these market services anytime you want.
So if you do get to the point where you say, hey, we have a specific investment property that we, that we have in mind, like we have a target property.
Well, you can click run and generate these comp set.

Um I'll let you know a big thing is that we have perfected collecting rent, specials and concessions.
They again do have to be on a property website, but as long as they're there, no matter what format we can collect them.

Um and we do take it a step further and we'll calculate net effective rent and that effective price per foot for you at the property level, at the floor plan level and all the way down to the actual unit level itself as well.

Um Is, is this something you look at nationally or do you have certain markets in mind?
And then also how many users are involved in that process?
I guess how many, how many analysts or, or who, who's now actually collecting and analyzing the data?

Alison is doing our packaging.

And OK, that makes sense.

But what I wanted to see you.
But yeah, what, what I wanted to show you is this units tab here, I was talking, we take the data all the way down to the actual unit level.

We'll show you days on market at the unit level.

So I like to show that to emphasize the importance of of the daily data that we are collecting.
But nonetheless, this is all downloadable to CS P or Excel as well.
So you can make your pivot tables or, or kind of do whatever you want or if you do want to live inside the app, we make it very easy to uh to manipulate.
